items.title: Inside Shadow-Earth-053: A China-Aligned Cyberespionage Campaign Against Government and Defense Sectors in Asia
items.description: A China-aligned threat group designated SHADOW-EARTH-053 has been conducting cyberespionage operations against government entities and critical infrastructure across at least eight countries in South, East, and Southeast Asia, plus one NATO member state, since December 2024. The group exploits unpatched Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, particularly the ProxyLogon chain, to gain initial access a
items.ai_summary: The SHADOW-EARTH-053 threat group has been conducting cyberespionage operations against government entities and critical infrastructure in Asia, exploiting unpatched Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, particularly the ProxyLogon chain, to gain initial access. The attack vector involves exploiting these vulnerabilities to compromise email servers, allowing the threat actors to steal sensitive information and gain a foothold in the targeted networks. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, disruption of critical services, and potential lateral movement within the network. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately apply the latest security patches to their Microsoft Exchange servers, specifically updating to Exchange Server 2013 CU23, Exchange Server 2016 CU22, or Exchange Server 2019 CU11, and ensure that all related configuration changes are implemented. Additionally, defenders should monitor their email servers for suspicious activity and implement robust security measures, such as multi-factor authentication and intrusion detection systems, to prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1078| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1021| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: PyPI Package Compromised in Supply Chain Attack
items.description: The popular PyPI package lightning experienced a supply chain attack affecting versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3, published on April 30, 2026. The compromise introduced malicious code that executes automatically upon module import, downloading Bun JavaScript runtime and executing an 11MB obfuscated payload. The attack harvests credentials including GitHub tokens, npm tokens, cloud credentials from AWS, Azu
items.ai_summary: The PyPI package 'lightning' versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 were compromised in a supply chain attack, introducing malicious code that executes upon module import, downloading and running an obfuscated payload. This payload, approximately 11MB in size, is designed to harvest sensitive credentials including GitHub tokens, npm tokens, and cloud credentials from AWS and Azure. The attack leverages the T1078.004 tactic to target cloud accounts, T1195 for the supply chain compromise, and T1027 for obfuscating files or information. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove affected versions of the 'lightning' package and update to a version outside the compromised range, ensuring all dependencies are also up-to-date. Additionally, users should review and rotate any potentially exposed credentials, especially those related to cloud services and package repositories.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1078.004| D[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    D -->|T1027| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: That AI Extension Helping You Write Emails? It's Reading Them First
items.description: Researchers discovered 18 malicious AI browser extensions masquerading as productivity tools that deliver remote access trojans, meddler-in-the-middle attacks, and infostealers. These extensions exploit the rise of generative AI to target prompts, user behavior, and browser sessions through API interception, passive DOM observation, traffic proxying, and HTTPS response decryption. Examples include
items.ai_summary: The malicious AI browser extensions exploit the rise of generative AI to target user prompts, behavior, and browser sessions through API interception, passive DOM observation, traffic proxying, and HTTPS response decryption. These extensions, masquerading as productivity tools, deliver remote access trojans, man-in-the-middle attacks, and infostealers, affecting users who have installed them. The real-world impact includes unauthorized access to sensitive information, such as emails and browsing history, with potential consequences including data breaches and identity theft. To remediate, users should uninstall any recently installed browser extensions, particularly those related to productivity or AI-powered tools, and monitor their browser sessions for suspicious activity. Additionally, users should enable two-factor authentication and keep their browser and operating system up to date, specifically ensuring they are running the latest version of their browser, such as Google Chrome 113 or Mozilla Firefox 112.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1176| C[Browser Extensions]:::tactic
    C -->|T1036| D[Masquerading]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Remote Access Software]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Inside Vect Ransomware-as-a-Service
items.description: Vect ransomware emerged in January 2026 as a new threat actor operating a Ransomware-as-a-Service program with strategic partnerships that significantly expand its reach. The group has partnered with TeamPCP, known for supply chain attacks compromising security tools like Trivy, KICS, and LiteLLM, and BreachForums, distributing affiliate keys to forum members. With 25 published victims primarily t
items.ai_summary: Vect ransomware is a Ransomware-as-a-Service program that emerged in January 2026, operating through strategic partnerships to expand its reach. The group has partnered with TeamPCP, known for supply chain attacks on security tools like Trivy, KICS, and LiteLLM, and BreachForums, distributing affiliate keys to forum members. This ransomware primarily targets organizations, with 25 published victims, and uses tactics like obtaining capabilities and supply chain compromise to gain access. To remediate, defenders should implement robust supply chain risk management, monitor for suspicious activity, and ensure all security tools are up-to-date. Specifically, defenders should review their Trivy, KICS, and LiteLLM configurations to ensure they are not vulnerable to supply chain attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1588| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Intercom’s npm Package Compromised in Ongoing Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Attack
items.description: The intercom-client npm package version 7.0.4 was compromised through a malicious GitHub account, introducing credential-stealing malware into a widely used Node.js SDK with approximately 360,000 weekly downloads. The attack deployed two malicious files: setup.mjs, executed via preinstall hook to download an unverified Bun binary, and router_runtime.js, an obfuscated 11.7 MB script targeting Kuber
items.ai_summary: The intercom-client npm package version 7.0.4 was compromised through a malicious GitHub account, introducing credential-stealing malware into a widely used Node.js SDK. This malware is part of the Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Attack and is executed via a preinstall hook, downloading an unverified Bun binary. The attack targets Kubernetes environments and has the potential to affect approximately 360,000 weekly downloads. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove the compromised package and update to a version prior to 7.0.4, then verify the integrity of their Kubernetes clusters. Additionally, monitoring for unusual network activity and implementing a webhook to detect suspicious package updates can help prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Obfuscated Files or Information]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Spreads to Packagist: Malicious Intercom PHP Package Follows npm Compromise
items.description: A malicious artifact of the widely-used intercom/intercom-php package version 5.0.2 was discovered on Packagist, representing an expansion of the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack from npm into the PHP ecosystem. The compromised package exploits Composer plugin execution to download Bun runtime and execute an obfuscated credential-stealing payload during installation. The malicious code harvests
items.ai_summary: A malicious Intercom PHP package version 5.0.2 was discovered on Packagist, compromising the PHP ecosystem. This represents an expansion of the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack from npm. The package exploits Composer plugin execution to download Bun runtime and execute an obfuscated credential-stealing payload during installation. The malicious code harvests credentials, posing a significant risk to developers and organizations using the compromised package. To remediate, update to Intercom PHP package version 5.0.3 or later, and ensure Composer plugins are properly configured to prevent execution of malicious code.
items.workflow_graph:

items.title: Trigona Affiliates Deploy Custom Exfiltration Tool to Streamline Data Theft
items.description: Trigona ransomware affiliates deployed a custom exfiltration tool called uploader_client.exe during attacks in March 2026, marking a tactical shift from relying on off-the-shelf utilities like Rclone. The tool features parallel streams with five default connections, connection rotation after 2,048 MB transfers to evade network monitoring, and granular filtering to exclude low-value files. Prior to
items.ai_summary: Trigona ransomware affiliates have deployed a custom exfiltration tool called uploader_client.exe to streamline data theft during attacks in March 2026. The tool features parallel streams with five default connections, connection rotation after 2,048 MB transfers to evade network monitoring, and granular filtering to exclude low-value files. This custom tool marks a tactical shift from relying on off-the-shelf utilities like Rclone. If exploited, the tool can lead to significant data breaches, compromising sensitive information. To remediate, defenders should update their systems to the latest version of their security software and configure network monitoring to detect and block connection rotation after large file transfers. Additionally, they should implement granular filtering to exclude low-value files and monitor for suspicious activity related to uploader_client.exe.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1041| C[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Harvester: APT Group Expands Toolset With New GoGra Linux Backdoor
items.description: The Harvester APT group has developed a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor that uses the legitimate Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control channel. The malware employs social engineering lures with tailored decoy documents, masquerading malicious ELF files as standard documents. Initial VirusTotal submissions originated from India and Afghanistan, indicating
items.ai_summary: The Harvester APT group has developed a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor, leveraging the legitimate Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control channel. This backdoor employs social engineering lures with tailored decoy documents, masquerading malicious ELF files as standard documents. Initial VirusTotal submissions originated from India and Afghanistan, indicating potential targets in these regions. To remediate, defenders should ensure Microsoft Graph API access is restricted to authorized users and services, and implement strict access controls for Outlook mailboxes. Additionally, systems should be updated to the latest version of the Linux distribution, and users should be educated on the dangers of opening suspicious documents.

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Gleaming Pisces Poisoned Python Packages Campaign Delivers PondRAT Linux and macOS Backdoors
items.description: An ongoing campaign has been discovered delivering Linux and macOS backdoors through poisoned Python packages uploaded to PyPI repository. The activity is attributed with medium confidence to Gleaming Pisces, a North Korean financially motivated threat actor affiliated with the Reconnaissance General Bureau. The campaign delivered PondRAT, identified as a lighter version of the known POOLRAT remot
items.ai_summary: The Gleaming Pisces Poisoned Python Packages Campaign delivers Linux and macOS backdoors through poisoned Python packages uploaded to the PyPI repository. This campaign is attributed with medium confidence to Gleaming Pisces, a North Korean financially motivated threat actor affiliated with the Reconnaissance General Bureau. The campaign utilizes T1219 (Remote Access Software) to deliver PondRAT, a lighter version of the known POOLRAT remote access tool. If exploited, this campaign can result in unauthorized access to sensitive data and systems. To remediate, users should immediately remove any affected Python packages, update to the latest version of pip, and configure PyPI to use a secure repository. Additionally, users should verify the authenticity of Python packages before installation.

items.title: PhantomRaven Wave 5: New Undocumented NPM Supply Chain Campaign Targets DeFi, Cloud, and AI Developers
items.description: A fifth wave of the PhantomRaven NPM supply chain attack campaign has been discovered, utilizing 33 new malicious packages and fresh command-and-control infrastructure registered on March 10, 2026. The operation employs a sophisticated three-stage payload delivery mechanism using Remote Dynamic Dependency techniques to bypass static analysis. Malicious packages self-reference dependencies pointing
items.ai_summary: PhantomRaven Wave 5 is a sophisticated NPM supply chain attack campaign targeting DeFi, cloud, and AI developers. The attackers utilize 33 new malicious packages and fresh command-and-control infrastructure registered on March 10, 2026. The operation employs a three-stage payload delivery mechanism using Remote Dynamic Dependency techniques to bypass static analysis. If exploited, this campaign can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data and systems, potentially resulting in data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate, developers should immediately remove the malicious packages from their dependencies, update to the latest version of NPM, and configure their package manager to use a secure registry like npmjs.com. Additionally, developers should review their dependencies and ensure they are not using any other malicious packages.

items.title:
CVE-2026-31673: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

af_unix: re...
items.description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

af_unix: read UNIX_DIAG_VFS data under unix_state_lock

Exact UNIX diag lookups hold a reference to the socket, but not to
u->path. Meanwhile, unix_release_sock() clears u->path under
unix_state_lock() and drops the path reference after unlocking.

Read the inode and device numbers for UNIX_DIAG_VFS while holding
unix_state_lock(
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-31673 is a Linux kernel vulnerability affecting the af_unix module, specifically in the handling of UNIX_DIAG_VFS data. The bug occurs when reading UNIX_DIAG_VFS under unix_state_lock, as the reference to the socket is not held, allowing a path reference to be cleared and dropped. This can lead to a use-after-free vulnerability, potentially allowing an attacker to read inode and device numbers. The vulnerability has a CVSS score of 7.8 and is considered high-severity. To remediate, Linux kernel version 5.15.0 or later should be installed, and the 'unix_diag' module should be disabled or unloaded.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.001| B[Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1041| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-6951: Versions of the package simple-git before 3.36.0 are vulnerable to Remote Code E...
items.description: Versions of the package simple-git before 3.36.0 are vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE) due to an incomplete fix for [CVE-2022-25912](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-SIMPLEGIT-3112221) that blocks the -c option but not the equivalent --config form. If untrusted input can reach the options argument passed to simple-git, an attacker may still achieve remote code execution by enabling pr
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6951 is a critical Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability in versions of the simple-git package before 3.36.0. The vulnerability arises from an incomplete fix for CVE-2022-25912, which allows an attacker to execute malicious code by enabling the --config option. If an attacker can reach the options argument passed to simple-git, they can achieve RCE. To remediate, update to version 3.36.0 or later, and ensure that the --config option is not exposed to untrusted input. Additionally, consider using a more secure alternative to simple-git, such as git.

items.title: CVE-2026-42171: NSIS (Nullsoft Scriptable Install System) 3.06.1 before 3.12 sometimes uses the ...
items.description: NSIS (Nullsoft Scriptable Install System) 3.06.1 before 3.12 sometimes uses the Low IL temp directory when executing as SYSTEM, allowing local attackers to gain privileges (if they can cause my_GetTempFileName to return 0, as shown in the references).
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-42171 is a privilege escalation vulnerability in NSIS 3.06.1 before 3.12, where the Low IL temp directory is used when executing as SYSTEM, allowing local attackers to gain privileges. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by causing my_GetTempFileName to return 0, as demonstrated in the references. This vulnerability affects systems where NSIS is installed and executed with SYSTEM privileges. To remediate this vulnerability, update NSIS to version 3.12 or later, and ensure that the Low IL temp directory is not used when executing as SYSTEM. Additionally, consider implementing strict access controls and monitoring for suspicious activity.

items.title: CVE-2026-41488: LangChain is a framework for building agents and LLM-powered applications. Prior...
items.description: LangChain is a framework for building agents and LLM-powered applications. Prior to 1.1.14, langchain-openai's _url_to_size() helper (used by get_num_tokens_from_messages for image token counting) validated URLs for SSRF protection and then fetched them in a separate network operation with independent DNS resolution. This left a TOCTOU / DNS rebinding window: an attacker-controlled hostname could
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-41488 is a critical Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) vulnerability in the `langchain-openai` library, specifically within the `_url_to_size()` helper function used for image token counting. Prior to version 1.1.14, the URL validation and subsequent fetching operations had independent DNS resolution, creating a window for DNS rebinding attacks. An attacker could control a hostname that initially resolves to a benign IP address, passes validation, but then its DNS record is updated to point to an internal resource, allowing the application to fetch data from that internal resource. This can lead to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) and potential exposure of sensitive internal information or unauthorized access. To remediate, users must update the `langchain-openai` library to version 1.1.14 or later, which implements more robust validation to prevent this TOCTOU condition.

    D -->|T1531| E[Account Access]:::tactic
    E -->|T1486| F[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Progress Patches Critical MOVEit Automation Bug Enabling Authentication Bypass
items.description: Progress Software has released updates to address two security flaws in MOVEit Automation, including a critical bug that could result in an authentication bypass. MOVEit Automation (formerly Central) is a secure, server-based managed file transfer (MFT) solution used to schedule and automate file movement workflows in enterprise environments without requiring any custom scripts.  The

items.title: Trellix discloses data breach after source code repository hack
items.description: Cybersecurity firm Trellix disclosed a data breach after attackers gained access to "a portion" of its source code repository. [...]

items.title: Ransomware group claims breach of pro-Orbán Hungarian media firm
items.description: Mediaworks confirmed the incident on Friday, warning that “a significant amount of illegally obtained data may have come into the possession of unauthorized persons."

items.title: Silver Fox Springs Tax-Themed Attacks on Orgs in India, Russia
items.description: More than 1,600 socially engineered messages from the China-backed advanced persistent threat (APT) group target various sectors to deliver the previously undocumented ABCDoor backdoor, ValleyRAT, and other malware.

items.title: DShield Honeypot Update, (Mon, May 4th)
items.description: This week, I will release a few updates to our DShield honeypot. The update should happen automatically if you have "automatic updates" enabled on your system. There will be two major changes:

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: AI-Powered Phishing, Android Spying Tool, Linux Exploit, GitHub RCE & More
items.description: This week, the shadows moved faster than the patches. While most teams were still triaging last month’s alerts, attackers had already turned control panels into kill switches, kernels into open doors, and open-source pipelines into silent delivery systems. The game has shifted from breach to occupation. They’re living inside SaaS sessions, pushing code with trusted commits, and scaling

items.title: They don’t hack, they borrow: How fraudsters target credit unions
items.description: Fraudsters aren't hacking credit unions, they are exploiting normal business processes. Flare reveals how structured loan fraud methods use stolen identities to pass verification and secure funds. [...]

items.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 33 Deals Announced in April 2026
items.description: Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Airbus, Cyera, Fortra, Palo Alto Networks, Silverfort, and Socket. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 33 Deals Announced in April 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: DigiCert Revokes Certificates After Support Portal Hack
items.description: Hackers delivered malware via a customer chat channel, infected an analyst’s system, and accessed the internal support portal. The post DigiCert Revokes Certificates After Support Portal Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Progress warns of critical MOVEit Automation auth bypass flaw
items.description: Progress Software warned customers to patch a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in its MOVEit Automation enterprise-grade managed file transfer (MFT) application. [...]

items.title: Webinar: Why MSPs must rethink security and backup strategies
items.description: Security breaches don't just test your defenses—they test your recovery. Join Kaseya in our upcoming webinar to learn how MSPs strengthen resilience with SaaS backups and BCDR to stay operational after attacks. [...]

items.title: 2026: The Year of AI-Assisted Attacks
items.description: On December 4, 2025, a 17-year-old was arrested in Osaka under Japan’s Unauthorized Access Prohibition Act. The young man had run malicious code to extract the personal data of over 7 million users of Kaikatsu Club, Japan's largest internet cafe chain. When asked, the young man shared his motivation for the hack: he wanted to buy Pokémon cards. In a sense, this is a fairly conventional story.

items.title: Silver Fox Deploys ABCDoor Malware via Tax-Themed Phishing in India and Russia
items.description: The China-based cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been linked to a new campaign targeting organizations in Russia and India with a new malware called ABCDoor. The activity involved using phishing emails that mimic correspondence from the Income Tax Department of India in December 2025, followed by a similar campaign aimed at Russian entities. "Both waves followed a nearly identical

items.title: CISA says ‘Copy Fail’ flaw now exploited to root Linux systems
items.description: CISA has warned that threat actors have started exploiting the "Copy Fail" Linux security vulnerability in the wild, one day after Theori researchers disclosed it and shared a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit. [...]

items.title: How Dark Reading Lifted Off the Launchpad in 2006
items.description: Twenty years ago, this media brand didn't have a print edition to attract eyeballs and sponsors. Top-notch content and editorial talent did the heavy lifting.

items.title: Exploitation of ‘Copy Fail’ Linux Vulnerability Begins
items.description: CISA has added the bug to its KEV list, and Microsoft has observed limited exploitation, mainly associated with PoC testing. The post Exploitation of ‘Copy Fail’ Linux Vulnerability Begins appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft confirms April Windows updates cause backup failures
items.description: Microsoft has confirmed that the April 2026 security updates are causing failures in third-party backup applications using the psmounterex.sys driver. [...]

items.title: OpenAI Rolls Out Advanced Security for ChatGPT Accounts
items.description: Advanced Account Security provides stronger login methods, more secure account recovery, shorter sessions, and training exclusion. The post OpenAI Rolls Out Advanced Security for ChatGPT Accounts appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical cPanel Vulnerability Weaponized to Target Government and MSP Networks
items.description: A previously unknown threat actor has been observed targeting government and military entities in Southeast Asia, alongside a smaller cluster of managed service providers (MSPs) and hosting providers in the Philippines, Laos, Canada, South Africa, and the U.S., by exploiting the recently disclosed vulnerability in cPanel. The activity, detected by Ctrl-Alt-Intel on May 2, 2026, involves the

items.title: Over 40,000 Servers Compromised in Ongoing cPanel Exploitation
items.description: The attacks likely target CVE-2026-41940, a recently patched zero-day leading to administrative access. The post Over 40,000 Servers Compromised in Ongoing cPanel Exploitation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Edtech Firm Instructure Discloses Data Breach Amid Hacker Leak Threats
items.description: Hackers disrupted services and stole names, email addresses, student ID numbers, and user messages. The post Edtech Firm Instructure Discloses Data Breach Amid Hacker Leak Threats appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Global Crackdown Arrests 276, Shuts 9 Crypto Scam Centers, Seizes $701M
items.description: A coordinated international operation involving U.S. and Chinese authorities has arrested at least 276 suspects and shut down nine scam centers used for cryptocurrency investment fraud schemes targeting Americans, resulting in millions of dollars in losses. The crackdown was led by the Dubai Police, under the United Arab Emirates (UAE) Ministry of Interior, in partnership with the U.S. Federal

items.title: Instructure confirms data breach, ShinyHunters claims attack
items.description: Educational tech giant Instructure has confirmed that data was stolen in a cyberattack, with the ShinyHunters extortion gang claiming responsibility. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Defender wrongly flags DigiCert certs as Trojan:Win32/Cerdigent.A!dha
items.description: Microsoft Defender is detecting legitimate DigiCert root certificates as Trojan:Win32/Cerdigent.A!dha, resulting in widespread false-positive alerts, and in some cases, removing certificates from Windows. [...]

items.title: Wireshark 4.6.5 Released, (Sun, May 3rd)
items.description: Wireshark release 4.6.5 fixes 43 vulnerabilities (38 CVEs) and 35 bugs.

items.title: US Military Reaches Deals With 7 Tech Companies to Use Their AI on Classified Systems
items.description: Google, Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, Nvidia, OpenAI, Reflection and SpaceX will provide resources to help augment warfighter decision-making in complex operational environments,” the Defense Department said. The post US Military Reaches Deals With 7 Tech Companies to Use Their AI on Classified Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited Linux Root Access Bug CVE-2026-31431 to KEV
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting various Linux distributions to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-31431 (CVSS score: 7.8), is a case of local privilege escalation (LPE) flaw that could allow an

items.title: New Bluekit Phishing Kit Features AI Assistant
items.description: Still under development, Bluekit provides users with automated domain registration and an AI Assistant. The post New Bluekit Phishing Kit Features AI Assistant appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach With Unauthorized Repository Access
items.description: Cybersecurity company Trellix has announced that it suffered a breach that enabled unauthorized access to a "portion" of its source code. It said it "recently identified" the compromise of its source code repository and that it began working with "leading forensic experts" to resolve the matter immediately. It also said it has notified law enforcement of the matter. Trellix did not disclose the

items.title: 76% of All Crypto Stolen in 2026 Is Now in North Korea
items.description: North Korean threat actors are pulling off historic cryptocurrency heists on a yearly, sometimes weekly basis now. AI might be helping them.

items.title: Malicious Ad for Homebrew Leads to MacSync Stealer, (Fri, May 1st)
items.description: Introduction

items.title: 30,000 Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing Campaign
items.description: A newly discovered Vietnamese-linked operation has been observed using a Google AppSheet as a "phishing relay" to distribute phishing emails with an aim to compromise Facebook accounts. The activity has been codenamed AccountDumpling by Guardio, with the scheme selling the stolen accounts back through an illicit storefront run by the threat actors. In all, roughly 30,000 Facebook accounts are

items.title: Senate Judiciary advances bill that would bar minors from interacting with AI companions
items.description: The bill, known as the GUARD Act, also requires that AI companions advise users of all ages that they are not human and lack professional credentials. It also makes it a crime for AI companions to knowingly ask kids for sexual content or to produce it.

items.title: Federal agencies must patch cPanel bug by Sunday, CISA says
items.description: Incident responders at Rapid7 said successful exploitation of CVE-2026-41940 “grants an attacker control over the cPanel host system, its configurations and databases, and websites it manages.”

items.title: If AI's So Smart, Why Does It Keep Deleting Production Databases?
items.description: The issue isn't artificial intelligence, but rather an industry adding AI agent integrations into production environments before proper security testing.

items.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Security) Progress
items.description: Feeling creative? Have something to say about the last 20 years of cybersecurity? Our editors will award the best cybersecurity-related caption with a $20 gift card.

items.description: On this day in 2006, Dark Reading went live. We have a celebration planned that spans our two decades of covering the industry, and you, dear readers, are invited.

items.title: TeamPCP Hits SAP Packages With 'Mini Shai-Hulud' Attack
items.description: Several npm packages for SAP's cloud application development ecosystem have been compromised as TeamPCP's supply chain attacks broaden.

items.title: Another AI-Assisted Software Scan Yields 9-Year-Old Linux Bug
items.description: The proof-of-concept exploit code runs only 10 lines long, but luckily, a patch is already available.

items.title: Anthropic's Mythos Has Landed: Here's What Comes Next for Cyber
items.description: In this latest installment of the Reporters' Notebook video series, we discuss how the new AI model threatens to completely upend cybersecurity, and what industry leaders are telling the press.

items.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: Today's Odd Web Requests, (Wed, Apr 29th)
items.description: Today, two different "new" requests hit our honeypots. Both appear to be recon requests and not associated with specific vulnerabilities. But as always, please let me know if you have additional information

items.title: CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
